meta.title: Provodnik Investor One-Pager
meta.subtitle: A lower-take marketplace for Russia's fragmented tours and excursions market.
meta.hero_copy: Provodnik is built for the part of the category incumbents do not organize well enough: custom traveler demand, budget-led group formation, and regional supply still selling through manual channels. The model combines listings with request-driven booking, structured guide offers, trust controls, and a target commission of 10% to 15%.

proof_items.copy: Tripster footprint documented in research: 955 cities across 120 countries.
Sputnik8 footprint documented in research: cities, countries, and guide base.
Sputnik8 public commission versus Provodnik target take rate.

strip_cards.label: Market Failure
strip_cards.copy: Discovery, trust, and group assembly still happen across Telegram, phone calls, social media, and referrals.

strip_cards.label: Wedge
strip_cards.copy: Travelers create demand directly, guides respond with structured offers, and groups form around dates and budget.

strip_cards.label: Launch Logic
strip_cards.copy: Start with trusted local supply and low-liability formats until request-to-booking liquidity becomes repeatable.

thesis.title: Executive Thesis
thesis.body:
Problem: travelers struggle to find the right excursion for budget, dates, format, and trust; guides rely on expensive aggregators or fragmented offline channels.

Wedge: Provodnik is not another catalog. It is a request-first marketplace where travelers can create demand, join others into groups, and receive structured offers from verified guides.

Why this can win: lower commission matters only when paired with new demand capture. Provodnik competes on liquidity for custom and budget-sensitive demand, not on listing breadth alone.

signals.label: Market Structure
signals.title: Incumbents validate the category
signals.copy: Large platforms already prove demand, booking behavior, reviews, response-time discipline, and trust mechanics.

signals.label: Underserved Segment
signals.title: The fragmented tail is still open
signals.copy: Custom requests, group assembly, and regional long-tail supply remain poorly digitized and operationally messy.

signals.label: Commercial Opening
signals.title: Lower take becomes meaningful
signals.copy: Research shows competitor guide economics leave room for a more attractive channel if demand quality is real.

why_now: Incumbents prove the market, but they optimize primarily for listing-first behavior.
Provodnik targets the less organized motion: traveler requests, budget-led group economics, and local guide supply outside polished catalog systems.
The MVP is transaction-complete: listings, requests, group joins, guide offers, deposits, reviews, moderation, and refunds.
Go-to-market is disciplined: launch with 1 to 3 regions, low-liability categories, and trusted local supply until liquidity repeats.

bottom_cards.title: Business Model
bottom_cards.body:
Revenue: commission on completed bookings.

Positioning: target take rate of 10% to 15% against higher documented incumbent economics.

Expansion: featured placement, professional tools, and analytics can layer on later.

bottom_cards.title: Go-to-Market
bottom_cards.body:
Start narrow: 1 to 3 regions with year-round demand and credible guide supply.

Prioritize: city tours, nature day trips, family-friendly small-group formats.

Avoid at launch: high-risk adventures, complex expeditions, and unclear regulatory segments.

bottom_cards.title: What Investors Should Watch
bottom_cards.body:
Core milestone: repeatable liquidity in a narrow wedge, not broad traffic.

Key signals: approved guides, traveler requests, time to first offer, request-to-booking conversion, cancellation and dispute rate.

Defensibility: more trusted guides, reviews, and grouped demand strengthen marketplace density.

footer: Source basis: PRD.md, MVP.md, MARKET_RESEARCH.md, README.md, and APP_STACK.md. All statements are derived from repo documentation and the March 12, 2026 research snapshot.
